description: Evaluate and cite appropriate primary and secondary sources to research the Pre-Columbian era through the Constitution.

subject: Social Studies
description: Construct and answer questions about the Pre-Columbian era through the Constitution based on multiple sources.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about history from before Columbus to the Constitution using many sources.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply the inquiry process to construct and answer historical questions.
description_plain: Your child uses questions and research to learn about history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare the impact of people, events, ideas, and symbols on various cultures and ethnic groups in the Pre-Columbian era through the Constitution.
description_plain: Your child can compare how people, events, ideas, and symbols affected different cultures and groups from before Columbus to the Constitution.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze past and current events and challenges from the Pre- Columbian era through the Constitution.

subject: Social Studies

description_plain: Your child learns how looking at history from many points of view helps us understand it better.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare and contrast primary and secondary sources to better understand multiple perspectives of the same event.
description_plain: Your child can compare different types of sources to understand different views of the same event.

subject: Social Studies

description_plain: Your child understands that history includes many perspectives, including those of groups often left out.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine the chronology of key events in the United States and communicate their impact on various groups in the past, present, and future.

subject: Social Studies
description: Investigate patterns of continuity and change over time from the Pre-Columbian era through the Constitution.
description_plain: Your child investigates how things have stayed the same or changed from before Columbus to the Constitution.

description_plain: Your child can use map and location skills to understand events and problems.

description_plain: Your child will learn to identify and show the features of flat and solid shapes.

description: Solve problems and reason with geometry using multiple representations, make connections within math and across disciplines, and communicate their ideas.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems with shapes, connect these ideas to other subjects, and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Solve authentic problems that involve addition and subtraction within 10 (e.g., by using objects, drawings, and equations to represent the problem).
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems by adding and subtracting numbers up to 10 using objects, drawings, or equations.

subject: Math
description: Efficiently, flexibly, and accurately add and subtract within 5.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and correctly add and subtract numbers up to 5.

subject: Math
description: For any number from 1 to 9, find the number that makes 10 when added to the given number, sharing the answer with a model, drawing, or equation.
description_plain: Your child can find the number that adds to another number to make 10 and show this with models, drawings, or equations.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose numbers less than or equal to 10 into pairs in more than one way using verbal explanations, objects, or drawings.
description_plain: Your child can break apart and put together numbers up to 10 in different ways using words, objects, or drawings.

subject: Math
description: Represent and explain addition and subtraction as part-whole relationships, with addition as putting together and/or adding to and subtraction as taking apart and/or taking from, using objects, drawings, numbers, and equations.
description_plain: Your child understands addition as putting things together and subtraction as taking things apart, and can show this with objects, drawings, numbers, and equations.

subject: Math
description: Number and Algebraic Relationships: Students will understand and demonstrate the meaning of addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child will learn what addition and subtraction mean and how to use them.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose numbers from 11 to 19 into a group of ten ones and some more ones using a model, drawing, or equation.
description_plain: Your child can break numbers from 11 to 19 into ten ones and some more ones using models, drawings, or equations.

subject: Math
description: Base Ten: Students will work with numbers 11 to 19 to gain a foundation for place value.
description_plain: Your child will work with numbers 11 to 19 to understand place value.

subject: Math
description: Compare the number of objects in two groups, up to 20, using the words fewer than, more than, the same as.
description_plain: Your child can compare two groups of up to 20 objects and say which has more, fewer, or the same.

subject: Math
description: Write and name numbers 0 to 20. Represent a number of objects with a written numeral 0 to 20.
description_plain: Your child can write and name numbers from 0 to 20 and match numbers to groups of objects.

subject: Math
description: Count verbally in sequential order by ones and by tens to 100, making accurate decade transitions (e.g., 89 to 90).
description_plain: Your child can count by ones and tens up to 100, including changing from one ten to the next (like 89 to 90).

subject: Math
description: Count verbally forward and backward from any given number within 20.
description_plain: Your child can count forward and backward within 20 starting from any number.

subject: Math
description: Count up to 20 objects arranged in a line, a rectangular array, or a circle, and count up to 10 objects in a scattered configuration.
description_plain: Your child can count up to 20 objects arranged in lines, rectangles, or circles, and up to 10 objects scattered around.

subject: Math
description: Count out the number of objects given a number from 1 to 20.
description_plain: Your child can count out a specific number of objects from 1 to 20.

subject: Math
description: Understand that each successive number name refers to a quantity that is one larger.
description_plain: Your child understands that each number name means one more than the number before it.

subject: Math
description: Use one-to-one correspondence when counting objects to show the relationship between numbers and quantities and understand the last number counted is a direct representation of the total objects in a given set.
description_plain: Your child can count objects one by one and knows the last number counted tells how many objects there are.

subject: Math
description: Counting and Cardinality: Students will understand the relationship between numbers and quantities.
description_plain: Your child will learn how numbers and amounts are connected.

subject: Math
description: Without counting, recognize and verbally label arrangements for briefly shown collections up to 10 (e.g., "I saw 5." "How did you know?" "I saw 3 and 2, that is 5."
description_plain: Your child can quickly recognize and say how many objects are shown up to 10 without counting each one.

subject: Math
description: Subitizing: Students will quantify briefly shown collections and verbally label the arrangements without counting.
description_plain: Your child will learn to recognize small groups of objects and say how many there are without counting.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems and reason with number concepts using multiple representations, make connections within math and across disciplines, and communicate their ideas.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems and think about numbers in different ways, connect math to other subjects, and explain their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Explain and justify mathematical ideas using precise mathematical language in written or oral communication.
description_plain: Your child can explain math ideas clearly using the right math words when writing or talking.

subject: Math
description: Analyze mathematical relationships to connect mathematical ideas.
description_plain: Your child can look at math problems and see how different math ideas connect.

subject: Math
description: Create and use representations to organize, record, and communicate mathematical ideas.
description_plain: Your child can make and use pictures, charts, or models to organize and share math ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason quantitatively and abstractly and consider the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and math ideas carefully and understand other people’s math thinking.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Ask and answer questions about the total number of data points, how many in each category, and compare categories by identifying how many more or less are in a particular category using a picture graph.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about how many items there are in total and in each group using a picture graph.

subject: Math
description: Analyze Data and Interpret Results: Students will analyze the data and interpret the results.
description_plain: Your child can look at data and explain what the information shows.

subject: Math
description: Collect, organize, and represent a data set with up to three categories using a picture graph.
description_plain: Your child can collect and organize data into up to three groups and show it using a picture graph.

subject: Math
description: Data Collection: Students will formulate questions to collect, organize, and represent data.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to gather information, organize it, and show it clearly.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems and reason with data/probability using multiple representations, make connections within math and across disciplines, and communicate their ideas.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems with data and chance using different ways to show their thinking and explain their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the half hour and hour using analog and digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write the time to the hour and half hour using both clocks with hands and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Count collections of like coins (penny, nickel, and dime) relating to patterns of counting by 1s, 5s, and 10s.
description_plain: Your child can count groups of pennies, nickels, and dimes by ones, fives, and tens.

subject: Math
description: Understand the value of dimes and pennies (e.g., a dime is equal to ten pennies) relating to tens and ones and solve problems involving dimes and pennies using the ¢ symbol appropriately.
description_plain: Your child understands that a dime is worth ten pennies and can solve problems using dimes and pennies with the ¢ symbol.

subject: Math
description: Time and Money: Students will solve problems with coins and tell time to the half hour.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems with coins and tell time to the half hour.

subject: Math
description: Order three objects by directly comparing their lengths or indirectly by using a third object.
description_plain: Your child can put three objects in order by length by comparing them directly or using another object.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object as a whole number of same-size, non-standard units by placing them end to end.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long something is by lining up same-size objects end to end and counting them.

subject: Math
description: Measurement: Students will measure and compare lengths.
description_plain: Your child can measure and compare lengths of objects.

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal parts using the language halves and fourths.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into two or four equal parts and use the words halves and fourths.

subject: Math
description: Describe lines and sides of shapes as parallel or non-parallel.
description_plain: Your child can describe if the sides or lines of shapes are parallel or not.

subject: Math
description: Determine geometric attributes of three-dimensional shapes including cones, cylinders, cubes, and rectangular prisms regardless of orientation or size.
description_plain: Your child can identify features of 3D shapes like cones, cylinders, cubes, and rectangular boxes no matter how they are turned or how big they are.

subject: Math
description: Determine geometric attributes of two-dimensional shapes regardless of orientation or size for rhombi, trapezoids, and hexagons (e.g., a hexagon is closed with six sides).
description_plain: Your child can identify features of 2D shapes like rhombus, trapezoid, and hexagon no matter how they are turned or sized.

subject: Math
description: Shapes and Their Attributes: Students will represent and describe the attributes of two-dimensional shapes.
description_plain: Your child can describe and show the parts of flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems and reason with geometry using multiple representations, make connections within math and across disciplines, and communicate their ideas.
description_plain: Your child can solve geometry problems using different ways to show their thinking and explain their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Create an authentic problem to represent a given equation involving addition and subtraction within 20.
description_plain: Your child can make up a real-life story that matches an addition or subtraction equation within 20.

subject: Math
description: Solve authentic problems involving addition and subtraction within 20 in situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all parts of the addition or subtraction problem by using objects, drawings, and/or equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems with addition and subtraction within 20 using objects, drawings, or equations with unknown numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems that call for addition of three whole numbers whose sum is less than or equal to 20 using flexible strategies with objects, drawings, and/or equations.
description_plain: Your child can add three whole numbers that total 20 or less using different strategies like objects or drawings.

subject: Math
description: Use the commutative property of addition to develop addition strategies and compose/decompose numbers to develop addition and subtraction strategies. (See other flexible strategies in 1.N.4.a49).
description_plain: Your child can use the idea that numbers can be switched in addition and break numbers apart to help add and subtract.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in an addition or subtraction equation (e.g., 7 + ? = 13).
description_plain: Your child can find the missing number in addition or subtraction problems like 7 + ? = 13.

subject: Math
description: Use the relationship of addition and subtraction to solve subtraction problems (e.g., find 12 – 9 = , using the addition fact 9 + 3 = 12).
description_plain: Your child can use addition facts to help solve subtraction problems, like knowing 9 + 3 = 12 helps find 12 – 9.

subject: Math
description: Use the meaning of the equal sign to determine if equations are true and give examples of equations that are true (e.g., 4 = 4, 6 = 7 – 1, 6 + 3 = 3 + 6, 7 + 2 = 5 + 4).
description_plain: Your child understands what the equal sign means and can tell if equations are true, like 4 = 4 or 6 + 3 = 3 + 6.

subject: Math
description: Number and Algebraic Relationships: Students will understand and apply properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can use addition and subtraction rules and the connection between them to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Subtract multiples of ten from two-digit numbers (positive or zero differences) using concrete models, drawings, and strategies that reflect an understanding of place value, the relationship between addition and subtraction, and the properties of operations. Relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used to solve.
description_plain: Your child can subtract tens from two-digit numbers using objects or drawings and explain how they solved it.

subject: Math
description: Understand that in adding two-digit numbers, one adds tens and tens, ones and ones; sometimes it is necessary to compose a ten.
description_plain: Your child knows that when adding two-digit numbers, you add tens to tens and ones to ones, and sometimes need to make a new ten.

subject: Math
description: Add within 100, including adding a two-digit number and a one-digit number, adding a two-digit number and a multiple of ten, using concrete models, drawings, and strategies that reflect an understanding of place value, the relationship between addition and subtraction, and the properties of operations. Relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used to solve.
description_plain: Your child can add numbers up to 100 using objects or drawings and explain how they did it.

subject: Math
description: Mentally find 10 more or 10 less than a two-digit number without having to count and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can quickly find 10 more or 10 less than a two-digit number without counting and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Find the difference between two numbers that are multiples of 10, ranging from 10 to 90 using concrete models, drawings, or strategies, and write the corresponding equation.
description_plain: Your child can find the difference between two multiples of 10 from 10 to 90 using objects or drawings and write the matching equation.

subject: Math
description: Efficiently, flexibly, and accurately add and subtract within 10.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers within 10 quickly and accurately.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 20, using flexible strategies such as counting on or counting back, making ten, using ten, and using doubles and near doubles.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract within 20 using different methods like counting on, making ten, or using doubles.

subject: Math
description: Number and Operations: Students will compute using addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers using different strategies.

subject: Math
description: Compare two, two-digit numbers using words greater than, less than, equal to, and symbols <, >, =. Justify comparisons based on the number of tens and ones.
description_plain: Your child can compare two two-digit numbers using words like greater than or less than and explain by looking at tens and ones.

subject: Math
description: Understand 10 as a bundle, collection, or (more abstractly) composition of ten ones and that the two digits of a two-digit number represent a composition of some tens and some ones.
description_plain: Your child understands that 10 is a group of ten ones and that two-digit numbers show how many tens and ones they have.

subject: Math
description: Base Ten: Students will represent and compare two-digit numbers to gain foundations for place value.
description_plain: Your child can show and compare two-digit numbers to understand place value.

subject: Math
description: Understand patterns of skip counting by 2s, 5s, and 10s.
description_plain: Your child can skip count by 2s, 5s, and 10s to recognize number patterns.

subject: Math
description: Write numerals to match a representation of a given set of objects for numbers up to 120.
description_plain: Your child can write numbers to match groups of objects up to 120.

subject: Math
description: Count verbally by ones and tens within 120 starting at any given number. Understand that the given number is a direct representation of the total objects in a given set and counting on each successive number represents adding an additional object, and counting back each proceeding number represents removing an object.
description_plain: Your child can count out loud by ones and tens up to 120 starting from any number and understand that counting forward adds objects and counting backward removes objects.

subject: Math
description: Count verbally by ones and tens within 120 starting at any given number.
description_plain: Your child can count out loud by ones and tens up to 120 starting from any number.

subject: Math
description: Counting and Cardinality: Students will understand the relationship between numbers and quantities to extend the counting sequence.
description_plain: Your child understands how numbers relate to amounts and can continue counting in order.

subject: Math
description: Without counting, recognize and verbally label arrangements for briefly shown collections up to 20 (e.g.," I saw 16." "How did you know?" "I saw 10 and 6, that is 16").
description_plain: Your child can quickly recognize and say how many objects are in a group up to 20 without counting each one.

subject: Math
description: Subitizing: Students will quantify briefly shown collections and verbally label the arrangements without counting.
description_plain: Your child can tell how many objects are in a group shown briefly without counting them one by one.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems and reason with number concepts using multiple representations, make connections within math and across disciplines, and communicate their ideas.
description_plain: Your child can solve number problems using different ways to show numbers, connect math ideas, and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Explain and justify mathematical ideas using precise mathematical language in written or oral communication.
description_plain: Your child can explain math ideas clearly using the right math words when writing or talking.

subject: Math
description: Analyze mathematical relationships to connect mathematical ideas.
description_plain: Your child can look at math problems and see how different math ideas connect.

subject: Math
description: Create and use representations to organize, record, and communicate mathematical ideas.
description_plain: Your child can make and use pictures, charts, or models to organize and share math ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason quantitatively and abstractly and consider the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and math ideas carefully and understand other people’s math thinking.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Analyze data using scaled picture graphs or bar graphs with up to four categories. Solve problems including one-step comparison problems, using information from the graphs.
description_plain: Your child can read and use picture or bar graphs with up to four groups to answer simple comparison questions.

subject: Math
description: Analyze Data and Interpret Results: Students will analyze the data and interpret the results.
description_plain: Your child can look at data and explain what it shows.

subject: Math
description: Create and represent a data set by making a line plot using whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot using whole numbers to show data.

subject: Math
description: Ask authentic questions to generate data and represent the data using bar graphs with up to four categories.
description_plain: Your child can ask real questions to collect data and show it using bar graphs with up to four groups.

subject: Math
description: Ask authentic questions to generate data and represent the data using scaled picture graphs with up to four categories.
description_plain: Your child can ask real questions to collect data and show it using picture graphs with up to four groups.

subject: Math
description: Data Collection: Students will formulate questions to collect, organize, and represent data.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, gather data, organize it, and show it in graphs.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems and reason with data/probability using multiple representations, make connections within math and across disciplines, and communicate their ideas.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems with data and chance using different ways to show information and explain their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Identify and write time to five-minute intervals using analog and digital clocks and both a.m. and p.m.
description_plain: Your child can tell time to the nearest five minutes on both analog and digital clocks, using a.m. and p.m.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies using $ and ¢ symbols appropriately.
description_plain: Your child can solve money problems using dollar bills and coins and write amounts with $ and ¢ symbols.

subject: Math
description: Time and Money: Students will solve problems with dollar bills and coins and tell time to the nearest five-minute interval.
description_plain: Your child can tell time to the nearest five minutes and solve problems with money using bills and coins.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve problems using the same standard-length units.
description_plain: Your child can use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve problems about lengths measured with the same units.

subject: Math
description: Represent whole numbers as equally spaced lengths on a number line diagram. Use number lines to find sums and differences within 100.
description_plain: Your child can use number lines to show numbers and find sums and differences within 100.

subject: Math
description: Relate Addition and Subtraction to Measurement: Students will add or subtract to solve length problems.
description_plain: Your child can add or subtract to solve problems about measuring length.

subject: Math
description: Measure and estimate lengths using whole numbers with inches, feet, centimeters, and meters.
description_plain: Your child can measure and estimate lengths using inches, feet, centimeters, and meters.

subject: Math
description: Identify and use appropriate tools for measuring length.
description_plain: Your child can pick and use the right tools to measure length.

subject: Math
description: Measurement: Students will use tools to measure and estimate length using standard units.
description_plain: Your child can use tools to measure and estimate length with standard units.

subject: Math
description: Compare the difference in length of objects using inches and feet or centimeters and meters.
description_plain: Your child can compare how long objects are using inches and feet or centimeters and meters.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object using two different length units and describe how the measurements relate to the size of the specific unit.
description_plain: Your child can measure the length of an object using two different units and explain how the measurements relate to the size of the units.

subject: Math
description: Describe Measurable Attributes: Students will measure, estimate, and compare lengths to build meaning of the measurement process.
description_plain: Your child can measure, estimate, and compare lengths to understand how measuring works.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape.
description_plain: Your child understands that equal parts of the same whole can look different in shape.

subject: Math
description: Divide circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal parts and describe the parts using the language of halves, thirds, fourths, half of, a third of, and a fourth of.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into 2, 3, or 4 equal parts and use words like halves, thirds, and fourths to describe them.

subject: Math
description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of equal-sized squares and count to find the total.
description_plain: Your child can divide a rectangle into equal squares arranged in rows and columns and count how many squares there are.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and draw two-dimensional shapes having a specific number of sides, angles, and vertices including triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons, and hexagons.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and draw flat shapes with a certain number of sides and corners, like triangles and hexagons.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and describe all faces of three-dimensional shapes as two-dimensional shapes. Identify and count attributes of solid shapes including the edges, faces, and vertices.
description_plain: Your child can identify the flat faces of 3D shapes and count their edges, faces, and corners.

subject: Math
description: Shapes and Their Attributes: Students will recognize and represent the attributes of two-dimensional shapes and three-dimensional solids.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and describe the parts of flat shapes and solid shapes.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems and reason with geometry using multiple representations, make connections within math and across disciplines, and communicate their ideas.
description_plain: Your child can solve geometry problems using different ways to show shapes, connect ideas, and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Identify a group of objects from 0 to 20 as even or odd by counting by 2s or by showing even numbers as a sum of two equal parts.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a group of up to 20 objects is even or odd by counting by 2s or showing even numbers as two equal parts.

subject: Math
description: Use repeated addition to find the total number of objects arranged in an array no larger than five rows and five columns and write an equation to express the total.
description_plain: Your child can use repeated addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rows and columns up to 5 by 5 and write an equation for it.

subject: Math
description: Create authentic problems to represent one-step addition and subtraction within 100 with unknowns in all positions.
description_plain: Your child can make up real problems that use one-step addition or subtraction within 100 with missing numbers in any position.

subject: Math
description: Solve authentic problems involving addition and subtraction within 100 in situations of addition and subtraction, including adding to, subtracting from, joining and separating, and comparing situations with unknowns in all positions using objects, models, drawings, verbal explanations, expressions, and equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve real addition and subtraction problems within 100 using objects, drawings, words, and equations, including problems where the missing number can be in any part of the problem.

subject: Math
description: Number and Algebraic Relationships: Students will create and solve problems involving addition and subtraction and work with equal groups of objects to gain foundations for multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can create and solve addition and subtraction problems and work with equal groups of objects to get ready for multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 1,000 using concrete models, drawings, and strategies that reflect an understanding of place value and the properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 1,000 using objects, drawings, and methods that show they understand place value and how addition and subtraction work.

subject: Math
description: Add up to three two-digit numbers using strategies based on place value and understanding of properties.
description_plain: Your child can add up to three two-digit numbers by using place value and number properties to help them.

subject: Math
description: Mentally add or subtract 10 or 100 to or from a given number 100 to 900.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add or subtract 10 or 100 to numbers between 100 and 900 in their head.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 100 strategies based on place value including properties of operations, relationships between addition and subtraction, and algorithms.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers within 100 using place value, understanding how addition and subtraction relate, and different methods.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 20.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately add and subtract numbers up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Number and Operations: Students will compute using addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child understands how to add and subtract numbers.

subject: Math
description: Compare two three-digit numbers by using symbols <, >, = and justify the comparison based on the value of the hundreds, tens, and ones.
description_plain: Your child can compare two three-digit numbers using <, >, or = and explain their answer by looking at hundreds, tens, and ones.

subject: Math
description: Understand 100 as a bundle, collection, or (more abstractly) composition of ten tens and that the three digits of a three-digit number represent a composition of some hundreds, some tens, and some ones.
description_plain: Your child understands that 100 is made of ten groups of ten, and that each digit in a three-digit number shows how many hundreds, tens, and ones there are.

subject: Math
description: Read and write numbers within the range of 0 to 1,000 using standard, word, and expanded forms.
description_plain: Your child can read and write numbers from 0 to 1,000 in normal form, words, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Base Ten: Students will represent and compare three-digit numbers to apply concepts of place value.
description_plain: Your child can show and compare three-digit numbers to understand place value.

subject: Math
description: Count within 1,000, including skip counting by 5s, 10s, and 100s starting at a variety of multiples of 5, 10, or 100.
description_plain: Your child can count up to 1,000, including skip counting by 5s, 10s, and 100s starting from different numbers.

subject: Math
description: Counting: Students will understand the relationship between numbers and quantities to extend the counting sequence.
description_plain: Your child understands how numbers and amounts relate and can keep counting higher.

subject: Math
description: Without counting, recognize and verbally label structured arrangements for briefly shown collections using groups, multiplicative thinking, and place value (e.g.," I saw 48." "How did you know?" "I saw 4 groups of 10 and 2 groups of 4 is 8...4 tens and 8 ones...48").
description_plain: Your child can quickly recognize and say how many are in a group shown briefly by noticing groups and place value without counting each one.

subject: Math
description: Subitizing: Students will quantify briefly shown collections and verbally label the arrangements without counting.
description_plain: Your child can look at a small group of items shown quickly and say how many there are without counting each one.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems and reason with number concepts using multiple representations, make connections within math and across disciplines, and communicate their ideas.
description_plain: Your child can solve number problems using different ways to show their thinking, connect math ideas, and explain their answers.

subject: Math
description: Explain and justify mathematical ideas using precise mathematical language in written or oral communication.
description_plain: Your child can explain math ideas clearly using the right math words when writing or talking.

subject: Math
description: Analyze mathematical relationships to connect mathematical ideas.
description_plain: Your child can look at math problems and see how different math ideas connect.

subject: Math
description: Create and use representations to organize, record, and communicate mathematical ideas.
description_plain: Your child can make and use pictures, charts, or models to organize and share math ideas.

subject: Math
description: Reason quantitatively and abstractly and consider the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can think about numbers and math ideas carefully and understand other people’s math thinking.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Analyze data and make simple statements using information represented in picture graphs, line plots, and bar graphs.
description_plain: Your child can look at picture graphs, line plots, and bar graphs and say simple things about the data.

subject: Math
description: Analyze Data and Interpret Results: Students will analyze the data and interpret the results.
description_plain: Your child can study data and explain what it shows.

subject: Math
description: Generate and represent data using line plots where the horizontal scale is marked off in halves and whole number units.
description_plain: Your child can make line plots that use halves and whole numbers on the scale to show data.

subject: Math
description: Create scaled picture graphs and scaled bar graphs to represent a data set with more than four categories, including data collected through observations, surveys, and experiments.
description_plain: Your child can create picture and bar graphs with scales to show data with more than four groups, using information from surveys, observations, or experiments.

subject: Math
description: Data Collection: Students will formulate questions to collect, organize, and represent data.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, collect data, organize it, and show it in graphs.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems and reason with data/probability using multiple representations, make connections within math and across disciplines, and communicate their ideas.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems and think about data and chance using different ways to show their ideas, connect math to other subjects, and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Solve authentic problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals and find elapsed time.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems by adding and subtracting time intervals and figuring out how much time has passed.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the minute using both analog and digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the exact minute using both clocks with hands and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Time: Students will tell time to the nearest minute and find elapsed time.
description_plain: Your child can tell time to the nearest minute and find out how much time has passed.

subject: Math

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply phonics and word analysis skills in decoding and encoding (spelling) words.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Delete part of a syllable within a spoken word including compound words (e.g., "Say 'parsnip.' Say it again but don't say 'par;' e.g., "Say 'cowboy.' Say it again but don't say 'cow'").

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can put together the beginning and ending parts of simple words to say the whole word.

description_plain: Your child shows they hear and play with sounds in words through talking activities.

subject: Language Arts

description: Use a recursive writing process to develop, strengthen, and produce writing appropriate to the audience, purpose, and discipline.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the central message or lesson in a literary text and explain how key details support that idea.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or lesson in a story and explain how important details show that message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough textual evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development of themes or central ideas in grade-level literary texts.
description_plain: Your child uses text evidence to explain how themes or main ideas develop in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade level high-frequency words with automaticity and accuracy (e.g., Fry or Dolch words or those included in instructional materials).

subject: Language Arts
description: Adjust pace and prosody based on the purpose, complexity, form, and/or style of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a variety of text accurately using appropriate rate, expression/prosody and intonation to reflect the meaning of text.
description_plain: Your child can read different kinds of texts clearly and with expression that matches the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop accuracy, phrasing, and expression/prosody while reading a variety of grade-level text to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts accurately and with good phrasing and expression to help understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level texts with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads fifth-grade texts accurately and smoothly to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multisyllabic words.
description_plain: Decode multisyllabic words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common derivational suffixes and describe how they turn words into different parts of speech.
description_plain: Your child can read words with common endings that change the word’s part of speech and explain how the endings do this.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words with common Latin endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply phonics and word analysis skills in decoding and encoding (spelling) words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses phonics and word study skills to read and spell words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate phonetic and word analysis knowledge and apply decoding skills to isolated words and in connected text.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to read words alone and in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate visual and/or digital tools to enhance verbal communication and add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, slides, or digital tools to make their speaking more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate awareness of and sensitivity to the appropriate use of words (e.g., stereotypes, multiple meanings of words).
description_plain: Your child understands how to use words carefully and respectfully, avoiding stereotypes and confusing meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the purpose and credibility of information being presented.
description_plain: Your child can tell why information is being shared and decide if it is trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Convey a perspective with clear reasoning and support.
description_plain: Your child can share their opinion clearly and give reasons to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate appropriate speaking techniques (e.g., appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, clear pronunciation) for a variety of purposes and situations, including interpreting 4th grade texts.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly using good eye contact, volume, and pronunciation for different situations and topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support themes or central ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence in which the organization, development, and style are appropriate to the discipline, audience, and/or context.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence in a way that fits the topic, audience, and situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Complete a task following multi-step directions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate active and attentive listening skills (e.g., eye contact, nonverbal cues, recalling, questioning).
description_plain: Your child listens carefully by making eye contact, using body language, remembering information, and asking questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Converse with peers and adults in an all-inclusive manner to foster positive relationships while respecting diverse perspectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate interpretation of verbal and non-verbal messages in a conversation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask relevant questions to build on ideas or acquire or confirm information.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate effectively and appropriately in collaborative activities for a variety of tasks, purposes, and audiences to express ideas, share knowledge, and generate new understandings.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases to connect ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use facts and details to support reasons and/or evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces that explain a perspective with supporting reasons and/or evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion related to the creative or expressive event or experience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use transitional words and phrases to organize a sequence of events that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can use transition words to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Write in a variety of modes for a variety of purposes and audiences across disciplines.

subject: Language Arts

description: Citing relevant and thorough evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development and interaction of individuals, ideas, and events in grade-level informational texts.

description: Analyze an individual, event, scientific idea or concept, or steps in a process.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central idea of an informational text and how it is conveyed through key details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough textual evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development of themes or central ideas in grade-level informational texts.

description_plain: Your child uses a step-by-step writing process to create and improve writing that fits the audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and revise fragment and run-on sentences and inappropriate shifts in verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child finds and fixes sentence fragments, run-on sentences, and incorrect verb tense changes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between and use types of adjectives (e.g., comparative, superlative).
description_plain: Your child knows and uses different types of adjectives, like comparing two or more things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of and use frequently occurring interjections, verb tenses (e.g., perfect), and correlative conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses common interjections, verb tenses, and paired conjunctions correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate an introductory element from the rest of a sentence, to separate clauses, to set off a question, and to indicate direct address.
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly to separate parts of sentences, questions, and when speaking directly to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of rules for capitalization; use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to indicate titles of works.
description_plain: Your child follows capitalization rules and uses underlining, quotation marks, or italics for titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create grammatically correct multi-paragraph compositions with varied sentence structures.
description_plain: Your child writes correct multi-paragraph pieces using different sentence types.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a recursive writing process to produce clear and coherent writing appropriate to the discipline, audience, and/or context.
description_plain: Your child uses steps to write clear and organized pieces that fit the topic and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of relationships between particular words (e.g., synonyms, antonyms, homographs) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related, like synonyms, opposites, and words spelled the same but with different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of commonly occurring idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child knows the meanings of common sayings, wise phrases, and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains similes and metaphors in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret an author's use of figurative, connotative, and technical language in grade-level literary and informational text.
description_plain: Your child understands an author’s use of special language like figurative and technical words in grade-level texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the precise meanings or pronunciations of words and phrases using reference materials and classroom resources.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries and other tools to find the exact meaning or pronunciation of words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commonly occurring Greek and Latin affixes and roots to determine the meanings of words.
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek and Latin word parts to figure out the meanings of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context clues (e.g., cause/effect relationships and comparisons in text) to determine the meanings of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the text to understand the meanings of words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use grade-level academic vocabulary appropriately.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses grade-appropriate academic words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build and use a range of conversational, academic, and discipline-specific grade-level vocabulary and apply to reading, writing, speaking, and listening.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses many words for talking, reading, writing, and listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend a wide range of informational text of appropriate complexity for Grade 5 independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands many kinds of informational texts on their own at a fifth-grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex, grade-level informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands difficult informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships between two or more individuals, events, ideas, or concepts in a range of informational texts.
description_plain: Your child explains how people, events, ideas, or concepts are connected in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the development of an author's claim(s) and how supporting evidence is used to support the claim(s).
description_plain: Your child looks at how an author builds their main point and uses evidence to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from multiple texts on the same topic in order to demonstrate knowledge of the topic.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from several texts about the same topic and show what they have learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough textual evidence to support ideas, evaluate how an author's perspective or use of point of view shapes the style and meaning of grade-level informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses text evidence to explain how an author's view changes the style and meaning of informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how text features (titles, headings, table of contents, glossaries, captions, graphs, maps, and/or other visuals) contribute to the meaning of texts.
description_plain: Your child understands how titles, headings, pictures, and other text features help explain the information in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the author's purpose(s) and describe how the author's perspective (e.g., beliefs, assumptions, biases) influences the meaning of an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why the author wrote the text and how the author's opinions or beliefs affect the information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development and interaction of individuals, ideas, and events in grade-level informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence to explain how people, ideas, and events develop and connect in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more individuals, events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in a process, drawing on supporting details from an informational text or texts.
description_plain: Your child can find similarities and differences between people, events, ideas, or steps in a process using details from texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the central idea in an informational text and how it is conveyed through key details.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main idea of an informational text and how important details support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough textual evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development of themes or central ideas in grade-level informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses text evidence to explain how main ideas or themes develop in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend a wide range of literary texts of appropriate complexity for Grade 5 independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read many different stories and books on their own and understand them well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex, grade-level literary texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands difficult stories on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships between two or more characters, events, or ideas in a range of literary texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how characters, events, or ideas are connected in stories and books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a literary text to answer and develop inferential questions to enhance the comprehension of self and others, quoting or paraphrasing specific evidence from the text.
description_plain: Your child can think about and answer questions about a story by using clues from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the treatment of themes and topics in literary texts of the same genre.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different stories of the same type handle similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough textual evidence to support ideas, evaluate how an author's perspective or use of point of view shapes the style and meaning of grade-level literary text.
description_plain: Your child uses text evidence to explain how an author's view changes the style and meaning of stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a sequence of chapters, scenes, or stanzas fit together to provide the overall structure of literary texts.
description_plain: Your child understands how chapters, scenes, or stanzas work together to create the whole story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a narrator or speaker's point of view influences the meaning of a literary text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the narrator’s or speaker’s point of view changes the meaning of a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development and interaction of individuals, ideas, and events in grade-level literary text.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence to explain how people, ideas, and events develop and connect in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a literary text or texts.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast characters, settings, or events in one or more stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the theme in a literary text and how it is conveyed through key details.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main message or theme of a story and how details help show it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough textual evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development of themes or central ideas in grade-level literary texts.
description_plain: Your child uses text evidence to explain how themes or main ideas develop in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adjust pace and prosody based on the purpose, complexity, form, and/or style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can change how fast or slow they read and how they use expression depending on the type and difficulty of the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a variety of texts accurately using appropriate rate, expression/prosody, and intonation to reflect meaning.
description_plain: Your child can read different kinds of texts clearly and with expression that shows the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop accuracy, phrasing, and expression/prosody while reading a variety of grade-level texts to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts accurately and with good phrasing and expression to help understand the story or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level texts with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads fifth-grade texts accurately and smoothly to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to decode unfamiliar multisyllabic words in and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use knowledge of sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new long words both in and out of context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Greek derived words.
description_plain: Your child can read words that come from Greek roots and parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply phonics and word analysis skills in decoding and encoding (spelling) words.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use sounds and word parts to read and spell words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate phonetic and word analysis knowledge and apply decoding skills to isolated words and in connected text.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to read words alone and in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate visual and/or digital tools to enhance verbal communication and add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, slides, or digital tools to make their speaking more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate awareness of and sensitivity to the appropriate use of words (e.g., stereotypes, connotations, subtleties of language).
description_plain: Your child understands how to choose words carefully to avoid stereotypes and show respect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose and credibility of information being presented.
description_plain: Your child can think about why information is shared and decide if it is trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Convey a perspective with clear reasoning and support.
description_plain: Your child can share their opinion clearly and explain their reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate and adjust speaking techniques (e.g., appropriate eye contact, pacing, adequate volume, clear pronunciation) for a variety of purposes and situations, including interpreting 6th grade texts.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly using good eye contact, volume, and pace for different situations and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically and using relevant descriptions, facts, and details to clarify themes or central ideas.
description_plain: Your child can present ideas in order, using facts and details to explain the main points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence in which the organization, development, and style are appropriate to the discipline, audience, and/or context.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence in a way that fits the topic, audience, and situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Complete a task following multi-step directions.
description_plain: Your child can follow directions that have several steps to complete a task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate active and attentive listening skills (e.g., eye contact, nonverbal cues, taking notes, summarizing, questioning).
description_plain: Your child listens carefully by making eye contact, noticing body language, taking notes, and asking questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Converse with diverse individuals in an all-inclusive manner to foster positive relationships while respecting diverse perspectives.
description_plain: Your child can talk with different people respectfully and include others’ ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate interpretation of verbal and non-verbal messages in a conversation.
description_plain: Your child understands both spoken words and body language during conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask relevant questions to build on ideas, clarify own ideas, or acquire or confirm information.
description_plain: Your child asks good questions to learn more, explain their ideas, or check information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate in structured discussions and collaborations about 6th grade topics and texts.
description_plain: Your child gets ready for and takes part in organized talks about 6th grade topics and books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate effectively and appropriately in collaborative activities for a variety of tasks, purposes, and audiences to express ideas, share knowledge, and generate new understandings.
description_plain: Your child works well with others to share ideas, learn new things, and complete different tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Practice safe and ethical behaviors when communicating and interacting with others digitally (e.g., safe information to share, utilize appropriate sites and materials, appropriate language use, respect diverse perspectives).
description_plain: Your child learns to use the internet and digital tools safely and respectfully when sharing information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate academic integrity by avoiding overreliance on any one source and referencing sources in writing and speaking; provide a list of sources using a standard format.
description_plain: Your child shows honesty by using many sources and giving credit to where information comes from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select and use appropriate note-taking formats to collect and organize information.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use ways to take notes that help organize information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate and evaluate credibility of evidence (e.g., motivation and/or potential bias of an information product) from print and digital sources to generate and answer questions and create new understandings.
description_plain: Your child can find and check if information is trustworthy to answer questions and learn new things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase and quote evidence to support ideas while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can put information into their own words or quote it correctly to support their ideas without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather and use credible evidence from trustworthy sources and assess its relevance in answering a research question.
description_plain: Your child can collect trustworthy information from good sources and decide if it helps answer their research question.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation(s).
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that matches the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions and key vocabulary to clarify relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use linking words and important vocabulary to show how ideas connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop a topic with information (e.g., facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, examples) related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, examples, and details to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and provide a general focus, grouping information logically and including text features, illustrations, and/or multimedia elements.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic and organize information in a logical way, using pictures, headings, or videos to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory pieces to examine a topic or text and clearly convey ideas and information.
description_plain: Your child will write informative pieces that explain a topic or text clearly with facts and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that sums up the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and key vocabulary to clarify the relationship between claim(s) and supporting evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and key vocabulary to show how their claim connects to the evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use relevant evidence from two or more credible sources.
description_plain: Your child uses important facts from two or more trustworthy sources to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a claim clearly and develop a structure in which the ideas are grouped logically.
description_plain: Your child clearly states their main idea and organizes supporting ideas in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments that explain a perspective with supporting reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that explain their point of view with reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that is clearly related to and appropriately reflects on the literary experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that clearly relates to the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, descriptive/sensory details, and figurative language to express personal or narrative voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words and phrases to signal shifts from one character, time frame, or setting to another.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use literary techniques (e.g. dialogue, pacing, description) to develop characters, events, settings, and conflicts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write in a variety of modes for a variety of purposes and audiences across disciplines.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate print and digital/multimedia tools to produce, enhance, and/or publish writing individually or in collaboration with peers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Improve and clarify writing by editing and proofreading, considering feedback from adults and peers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Improve and clarify the content, structure, and organization of writing by revising, considering feedback from adults and peers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt writing processes to sustain engagement in short and long-term writing tasks of increasing length and complexity.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use prewriting activities and inquiry tools to plan, organize, and draft writing.
description_plain: Your child uses planning activities and questions to organize and start their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a recursive writing process to develop, strengthen, and produce writing appropriate to the audience, purpose, and discipline.
description_plain: Your child uses a step-by-step writing process to create and improve writing that fits the audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and revise fragment and run-on sentences and inappropriate shifts in verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can find and fix sentence fragments, run-on sentences, and wrong verb tense changes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between and use different types of phrases (e.g., prepositional and appositive).
description_plain: Your child knows different types of phrases and uses them correctly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and use verb tenses (e.g., progressive).

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a colon to introduce items in a series; use a semicolon to combine independent clauses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (e.g., commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off non-restrictive clauses.
description_plain: Your child uses punctuation like commas, parentheses, and dashes to set off extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of rules for capitalization.
description_plain: Your child applies capitalization rules correctly in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create grammatically correct multi-paragraph compositions with varied sentence structures.
description_plain: Your child writes multi-paragraph pieces with correct grammar and varied sentence types.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a recursive writing process to produce clear and coherent writing appropriate to the discipline, audience, and/or context.
description_plain: Your child uses steps to write clear and organized pieces that fit the topic and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between the connotations of words with similar denotations (e.g., economical, thrifty).
description_plain: Your child understands the different feelings or ideas behind words that have similar meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the relationship between words (e.g., cause/effect, part/whole, item/category).
description_plain: Your child figures out how words are related, like cause and effect or part and whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., literary, biblical, or mythological allusions) in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figures of speech and references in stories or texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret an author's use of figurative, connotative, and technical language in grade-level literary and informational text.
description_plain: Your child understands how authors use special language like metaphors, feelings, and technical words in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials to determine or clarify the precise meanings, pronunciations, or parts of speech of words.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries or other resources to find exact word meanings, pronunciations, or parts of speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commonly occurring Greek and Latin affixes and roots to determine the meanings of words (e.g., audience, audible).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek and Latin roots and prefixes to figure out word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context clues (e.g. the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) to determine the meanings of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from sentences or paragraphs to understand the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate grade-level academic vocabulary appropriately for a variety of tasks and purposes.
description_plain: Your child uses grade-level academic words correctly for different tasks and purposes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build and use a range of conversational, academic, and discipline-specific grade-level vocabulary and apply to reading, writing, speaking, and listening.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses many words for talking, reading, writing, and listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend a wide range of informational texts of appropriate complexity for the 6-8 grade band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.

subject: Language Arts

description: Compare and contrast one author's presentation of information with that of another.
description_plain: Your child compares how two authors present information differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough textual evidence to support ideas, evaluate how an author's perspective or use of point of view shapes the style and meaning of grade-level informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses text evidence to explain how an author's view changes the style and meaning of informational texts.

description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author establishes and conveys a perspective or purpose in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows their point of view or purpose in a nonfiction text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development and interaction of individuals, ideas, and events in grade-level informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence to explain how people, ideas, and events develop and connect in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a key individual, event, or idea or concept is introduced and developed, drawing on specific supporting details in an informational text.

description: Determine the implied or explicit central idea of an informational text and how it develops over the course of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough textual evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development of themes or central ideas in grade-level informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses text evidence to explain how main ideas or themes develop in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child uses text evidence to explain how an author's view changes the style and meaning of stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author establishes and conveys the point(s) of view of a narrator or speaker in a literary text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development and interaction of individuals, ideas, and events in grade-level literary text.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence to explain how people, ideas, and events develop and connect in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a plot unfolds as well as how the characters respond to events or changes as the plot moves toward a resolution.
