description: Determine the implied or explicit theme of a literary text and how it develops over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands the main message or theme of a story and how it develops throughout the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough textual evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development of themes or central ideas in grade-level literary texts.
description_plain: Your child uses text evidence to explain how themes or main ideas develop in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate visual and/or digital tools to enhance verbal communication and add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, slides, or digital tools to make their speaking more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate awareness of and sensitivity to the appropriate use of words (e.g., stereotypes, multiple meanings of words).
description_plain: Your child understands how to choose words carefully to avoid stereotypes and confusing meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose and credibility of information being presented.
description_plain: Your child can think about why information is being shared and decide if it is trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Convey a perspective with clear reasoning and valid evidence.
description_plain: Your child can share their opinion clearly and support it with good reasons and facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate and adjust speaking techniques (e.g., appropriate eye contact, pacing, adequate volume, clear pronunciation) for a variety of purposes and situations, including interpreting 7th grade texts.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly using good eye contact, volume, and pace for different situations and topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing key ideas in a focused, coherent manner with relevant descriptions, facts, details, and examples to clarify themes or central ideas.
description_plain: Your child can present ideas and findings clearly, using facts and examples to explain the main points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence in which the organization, development, and style are appropriate to the discipline, audience, and/or context.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence in a way that fits the topic, audience, and situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Complete a task following multi-step directions.
description_plain: Your child can follow directions that have several steps to complete a task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate active and attentive listening skills (e.g., eye contact, nonverbal cues, taking notes, summarizing, questioning).
description_plain: Your child listens carefully by making eye contact, using body language, taking notes, and asking questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Converse with peers and adults in an all-inclusive manner to foster positive relationships while respecting diverse perspectives.
description_plain: Your child talks with others respectfully and includes different viewpoints to build good relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate interpretation of verbal and non-verbal messages in a conversation.
description_plain: Your child understands both spoken words and body language during conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask relevant questions to build on ideas, clarify own ideas, or acquire or confirm information.
description_plain: Your child asks questions that help them understand better or add to the conversation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate in structured discussions and collaborations about 7th grade topics and texts.
description_plain: Your child gets ready for and takes part in organized talks about 7th grade topics and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate effectively and appropriately in collaborative activities for a variety of tasks, purposes, and audiences to express ideas, share knowledge, and generate new understandings.
description_plain: Your child works well with others to share ideas, learn new things, and complete different tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Practice safe and ethical behaviors when communicating and interacting with others digitally (e.g., safe information to share, utilize appropriate sites and materials, appropriate language use, respect diverse perspectives).
description_plain: Your child practices safe and respectful behavior when using the internet and digital tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate academic integrity by avoiding overreliance on any one source and citing sources within text (e.g., parenthetical and numerical); provide a list of sources using a standard format.
description_plain: Your child shows honesty by not relying too much on one source and by giving credit to where information comes from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select and use appropriate note-taking formats to collect and organize information.
description_plain: Your child uses different ways to take notes and organize information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate and evaluate the credibility of evidence (e.g., motivation and/or potential bias of an information product) from print and digital sources to generate and answer questions and create new understandings.
description_plain: Your child finds and checks if information from books or the internet is trustworthy to answer questions and learn new things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate evidence into writing by quoting or paraphrasing data and conclusions while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child includes facts and ideas from sources in their writing by quoting or putting it in their own words without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather and use credible evidence from multiple trustworthy sources and assess its relevance in answering the research question.
description_plain: Your child gathers trustworthy information from many sources and decides if it helps answer their research question.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation(s).
description_plain: Your child writes a closing sentence or paragraph that sums up the information or explanation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions and key vocabulary to clarify relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words and important vocabulary to show how ideas connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop a topic with information (e.g., facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, examples) related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, examples, and quotes to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and provide a specific focus, grouping information logically and including text features, illustrations, and/or multimedia elements.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly and organizes information logically, using pictures or multimedia when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory pieces to examine a topic or text and clearly convey ideas and information.
description_plain: Your child writes informative pieces that explain a topic or text clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument(s) presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and key vocabulary to create cohesion and clarify the relationship between claim(s) and supporting evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect their claim with supporting evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain and cite relevant evidence from multiple credible sources.
description_plain: Your child explains and cites evidence from several trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop a structure to sequence ideas appropriately; introduce a clear claim where appropriate.
description_plain: Your child organizes ideas in a clear order and states their claim when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments that develop a perspective with supporting reasons and evidence, organized as appropriate to the task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that share their viewpoint with reasons and evidence, organized for the audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that is clearly related to and appropriately reflects on the literary experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that relates to and reflects on their reading or experiences with stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, descriptive/sensory details, and figurative language to express personal or narrative voice.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words and phrases to signal shifts from one character, time frame, or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to show changes between characters, times, or places in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use literary techniques (e.g., dialogue, pacing, description) to develop characters, events, settings, and conflicts.

description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or character(s), establishing and maintaining a point of view, and organizing an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write in a variety of modes for a variety of purposes and audiences across disciplines.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate print and digital/multimedia tools to produce, enhance, and/or publish writing individually or in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: Your child can use print and digital tools to create, improve, and share their writing alone or with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Improve and clarify writing by editing and proofreading, considering feedback from adults and peers.
description_plain: Your child can edit and proofread their writing to make it clearer, using helpful feedback from adults and friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Improve and clarify the content, structure, and organization of writing by revising, considering feedback from adults and peers.
description_plain: Your child can revise their writing to improve the content, structure, and organization by thinking about feedback from adults and peers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt writing processes to sustain engagement in short and long-term writing tasks of increasing length and complexity.
description_plain: Your child can adjust their writing process to stay focused and complete both short and longer, more complex writing tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use prewriting activities and inquiry tools to plan, organize, and draft writing.
description_plain: Your child can use planning activities and questions to organize and start their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a recursive writing process to develop, strengthen, and produce writing appropriate to the audience, purpose, and discipline.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use a step-by-step writing process to create and improve writing that fits the audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and revise fragment and run-on sentences and inappropriate shifts in verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can find and fix sentence fragments, run-on sentences, and incorrect verb tense changes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of prepositional and appositive phrases in sentences and paragraphs.
description_plain: Your child can use different types of phrases, like prepositional and appositive phrases, correctly in sentences and paragraphs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between and use types of clauses (e.g., noun, relative, adverbial), modifiers (e.g., misplaced and dangling), and adjectives (coordinate and cumulative).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use different types of clauses, modifiers, and adjectives correctly in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate coordinate adjectives.
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly to separate adjectives that work together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of rules for capitalization.
description_plain: Your child can apply capitalization rules correctly in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create grammatically correct multi-paragraph compositions with varied sentence structures.
description_plain: Your child can write clear, multi-paragraph pieces using a variety of sentence types and correct grammar.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a recursive writing process to produce clear and coherent writing appropriate to the discipline, audience, and/or context.
description_plain: Your child uses steps to write clear and organized pieces that fit the topic and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between the connotations of words with similar denotations (e.g., polite, diplomatic).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the feelings or ideas behind words that have similar meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the relationship between words (e.g., cause/effect, part/whole, item/category).
description_plain: Your child can understand how words are related, like cause and effect or part and whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., literary, biblical, or mythological allusions) in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech, like references to stories or myths, based on the context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret an author's use of figurative, connotative, and technical language in grade-level literary and informational text.
description_plain: Your child can understand how authors use special language like figurative or technical words in grade-level reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials to determine or clarify the precise meanings, pronunciations, or parts of speech of words.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources to find the exact meaning, pronunciation, or part of speech of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commonly occurring Greek and Latin affixes and roots to determine the meanings of words (e.g., audience, audible).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek and Latin roots and prefixes to figure out the meanings of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context clues (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) to determine the meanings of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence or paragraph to understand the meaning of unfamiliar words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate grade-level academic vocabulary appropriately for a variety of tasks and purposes.
description_plain: Your child can use grade-level academic words correctly for different tasks and purposes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build and use a range of conversational, academic, and discipline-specific grade-level vocabulary and apply to reading, writing, speaking, and listening.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses many words for talking, reading, writing, and listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend a wide range of informational texts of appropriate complexity for the 6-8 grade band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand many kinds of informational texts at a 6-8 grade level, with some help for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex, grade-level informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands difficult informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast regional, national, and/or multicultural perspectives by explaining how an author or narrator/speaker introduces, illustrates, or describes characters or individuals, events, and ideas within and across informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can compare different cultural or regional views by explaining how authors describe people, events, and ideas in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the development of an argument and identify the type(s) of reasoning used to support the argument.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how an author builds an argument and identify the types of reasons used to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast how two or more authors provide conflicting information on the same topic, including where the texts disagree on matters of evidence or interpretation.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more authors give different information or opinions about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child uses text evidence to explain how an author's view changes the style and meaning of informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the major sections of text contribute to the development of ideas in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the main parts of an informational text help develop its ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author establishes or conveys a perspective or purpose and distinguishes it from that of others.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how an author shows their viewpoint or purpose and how it differs from others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development and interaction of individuals, ideas, and events in grade-level informational texts.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence to explain how people, ideas, and events develop and connect in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationships and interactions between individuals, events, and/or ideas or concepts, drawing on specific supporting details in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more implied or explicit central ideas of an informational text and how they are supported with key details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough textual evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development of themes or central ideas in grade-level informational texts.

description: Compare and contrast regional, national, and/or multicultural perspectives by explaining how an author or narrator/speaker introduces, illustrates, or describes characters or individuals, events, and ideas within and across literary texts.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child uses text evidence to explain how an author's view changes the style and meaning of stories.

description: Citing relevant and thorough evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development and interaction of individuals, ideas, and events in grade-level literary text.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence to explain how people, ideas, and events develop and connect in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular events, lines of dialogue, or descriptive details develop the plot, reveal aspects of characters, or create meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more implied or explicit themes in a literary text and how they are supported with key details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Citing relevant and thorough textual evidence to support ideas, evaluate the development of themes or central ideas in grade-level literary texts.
description_plain: Your child uses text evidence to explain how themes or main ideas develop in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select and use appropriate visual and/or digital tools to enhance verbal communication and add interest.

description: Practice safe and ethical behaviors when communicating and interacting with others digitally (e.g., safe information to share, utilize appropriate sites and materials, appropriate language use, respect diverse perspectives).

description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and supports the argument(s) presented.

description: Identify and revise fragment and run-on sentences and inappropriate shifts in verb tense, number, voice, and mood.

description: Explain the function of and use different types of verbals in sentences (e.g., gerunds, participles, infinitives).

description: Use a recursive writing process to produce clear and coherent writing appropriate to the discipline, audience, and/or context.

description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
